title: Content Containers — Box Model
description: Now that your base styles are set, it's time to structure the page using the CSS box model. Add a .container class to center and constrain content width, apply the universal box-sizing: border-box rule so padding never breaks layouts, and style .post cards with padding, margin, background, and a border.
test_code:
assert 'box-sizing' in css.lower(), 'Missing box-sizing property'
assert 'border-box' in css.lower(), 'Should use border-box value for box-sizing'
assert '.container' in css.lower(), 'Missing .container selector'
assert 'max-width' in css.lower(), 'Missing max-width on .container'
assert 'margin' in css.lower(), 'Missing margin (use auto to center .container)'
assert '.post' in css.lower(), 'Missing .post selector'
assert 'padding' in css.lower(), 'Missing padding property'
acceptance_criteria: Universal box-sizing: border-box rule is applied
.container has max-width, margin: auto for centering, and padding
.post has padding, margin-bottom, background, and a border
hints: Use *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; } for the universal rule
A good max-width for a blog container is 900px to 1200px
margin: 0 auto centers a block element horizontally
